994.reason: [Potential IndexOutOfBoundsException: lexer.getToken(idx) may access beyond token list if no ';' is found, and the loop does not check lexer.notEnd() for idx offset]

2321.reason: [The condition uses String.contains() which will match a single '|' as well, incorrectly treating it as the operator. This may lead to false acceptance of a malformed '|' token, which should be flagged as error type 'a'. Should use equals("||") instead.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition uses String.contains() which will match a single '|' as well, incorrectly treating it as the operator. This may lead to false acceptance of a malformed '|' token, which should be flagged as error type 'a'. Should use equals("||") instead.]

523.reason: The Lexer's init() method accesses srccode.charAt(p+1) without bounds check when encountering '|' or '&' at the end of the string, causing a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException at runtime.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The Lexer's init() method accesses srccode.charAt(p+1) without bounds check when encountering '|' or '&' at the end of the string, causing a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException at runtime.

2118.reason: [Long.getLong() is used to retrieve system properties and returns null for numeric strings, which causes a NullPointerException. It should be replaced with Long.parseLong() or Long.valueOf() to correctly parse the string into a long value.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Long.getLong() retrieves a system property by name instead of parsing a numeric string; should use Long.parseLong().
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Long.getLong() is used to retrieve system properties and returns null for numeric strings, which causes a NullPointerException. It should be replaced with Long.parseLong() or Long.valueOf() to correctly parse the string into a long value.]

1004.reason: [Incorrect lookahead: should check if the next token after identifier is '(', but this checks two tokens ahead without verifying the intermediate token is an identifier]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This check is unsafe because it assumes there are at least 3 tokens ahead, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException when the input ends early. Also, FuncDef can have 'void' return type which is not handled here.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead: should check if the next token after identifier is '(', but this checks two tokens ahead without verifying the intermediate token is an identifier]

1010.reason: [This branch handles non-'const'/'int'/'char' tokens by assuming they are FuncDefs, but according to grammar, CompUnit only starts with Decl or FuncDef or MainFuncDef. This could incorrectly parse invalid tokens as FuncDef.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This branch handles non-'const'/'int'/'char' tokens by assuming they are FuncDefs, but according to grammar, CompUnit only starts with Decl or FuncDef or MainFuncDef. This could incorrectly parse invalid tokens as FuncDef.]

1014.reason: [The "<CompUnit>" should be printed before processing its components according to the problem requirement: "Before completing the analysis of any syntax components... output the name of the current syntax component". But here it's printed after all processing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The "<CompUnit>" should be printed before processing its components according to the problem requirement: "Before completing the analysis of any syntax components... output the name of the current syntax component".
